description: Aquatic plants grow upward/nearby in water.
Thin biofilm/lichen colonizes damp surfaces; patch attachment.
Organic layer composts into humus over time.
Live coral requires water; dries into skeleton; can regrow in water.
Mycelium/fungal duff spreads through organic substrates.
Layered detritus accumulates from sources (leaf fall, spores, sediment).
Moss spreads to adjacent suitable surfaces under humidity.
